rulings.Case Title: LEAH AHN VS. PRIYA SANGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Intention And Motion To Vacate And Enter Different Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS PRIYA SANGER, MICHAEL SANGER'S Motion To Vacate And Enter Different Judgment The hearing will be at 9:00 a.m. before Judge Kahn. The motion is denied. The moving party has failed to provide sufficient grounds for a different decision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Ahn is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel C.I. Energia Solar S.A. To Further Respond To Swinerton Builders Request For Production Of Documents Set 1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2019, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT SWINERTON BUILDERS' Motion To Compel C.I. Energia Solar S.A. To Further Respond To Swinerton Builders Request For Production Of Documents Set 1 Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties are ordered to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA L. HUDNALL VS. HINES GS PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-556980, CGC-18-571649 WITH CASE CGC-16-551921
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PATRICIA HUDNALL'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-556980, CGC-18-571649 WITH CASE CGC-16-551921 Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate Case Nos. CGC 16-551921, 17-556980 and 18-571649 per CCP §1048 is GRANTED. Common questions of fact and law are pending before the court in these cases brought by four Environmental Protection Agency employees. All allege maladies from airborne toxins in their place of work at 75 Hawthorne Plaza related to a large-scale renovation. Plaintiffs might have moved earlier, but judicial economy will be served and inconsistent adjudications avoided, and consolidation is discretionary. (Todd-Stenberg v. Dalkon Shield Claimants Trust (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 976, 978.) Defendants argue distinct exposures and symptoms, but that goes without saying as plaintiffs are distinct people. Given the high intelligence of San Francisco jurors, they would not be confused by four plaintiffs, were the consolidated cases ever to be tried. Defendants also argue that the status of discovery varies among the three cases, but show no real prejudice. Consolidation is for all purposes; 16-551921 is the lead case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BARRETT BUSINESS SERVICES, INC. VS. ORACLE AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion To Dismiss Basbd On Forum Non Conveniens, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Andrew C. Shen
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS COGNIZANT WORLDWIDE LIMITED, COGNIZANT TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS CORPORATION'S Motion To Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens Defendants Cognizant Worldwide Ltd. and Cognizant Technology Solutions Corp.'s motion to dismiss based on forum non conveniens is GRANTED. A mandatory forum-selection clause "will ordinarily be given effect without any analysis of convenience." (Intershop Communications v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 191, 196.) It need not be determined whether the forum-selection clause in the master services agreement (New York) or the statement of work (Washington) applies; both are mandatory clauses that require dismissal of plaintiff Barrett Business Services, Inc.'s claims against the Cognizant entities in California. Intershop provides that a mandatory forum-selection clause need not say the forum is "exclusive;" rather, use of "the" connotes "a single place" agreed to be the dispute-resolution forum. (Id.) Whether the exclusive forum is New York or Washington, it is not California. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BARRETT BUSINESS SERVICES, INC. VS. ORACLE AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Unopposed Motion, Unopposed Motion To File Under Seal, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Andrew C. Shen
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 6, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS COGNIZANT WORLDWIDE LIMITED, COGNIZANT TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS CORPORATION'S Motion To File Under Seal Defendants Cognizant Worldwide Ltd. and Cognizant Technology Solutions Corp.'s unopposed motion to file under seal is GRANTED. Defendants identify an interest that overcomes public access to the records and demonstrate that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored to protect that interest. (CRC 2.550.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
